'''Wes Davies''' (born 20 January 1978) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer, playing international level rugby league for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]]. At club level, he played rugby league for the [[Wigan Warriors]] and  [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], and rugby union for [[Orrell R.U.F.C.]], [[Worcester Warriors]], [[Doncaster Knights]] and the [[Cornish Pirates]] in the [[RFU Championship]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org (RL)"/><ref>{{cite web|title=Wes Davies – Wing|url=http://cornish-pirates.com/11-12_player_profiles/wes_davies.html|publisher=Cornish Pirates|accessdate=5 June 2012}}</ref>

==Background==
Wes Davies was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England, he is the grandson of the rugby union and rugby league footballer; [[Billy Boston]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Grandad is happy |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/2426622/Grandad-is-happy.html |website=The Telegraph |access-date=20 January 2024 |date=8 December 2003}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Davies started his career as a [[rugby league]] player with [[Wigan Warriors]]. In October 2001, he switched codes to [[rugby union]], joining [[Orrell R.U.F.C.|Orrell]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Davies latest to make union switch |url=http://en.espn.co.uk/scrum/rugby/story/38830.html |website=ESPN |access-date=20 January 2024 |date=8 October 2001}}</ref> He had a brief second spell in rugby league with [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in 2003 before returning to rugby union with [[Worcester Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Warriors snap up two backs |url=https://www.worcesternews.co.uk/news/7604009.warriors-snap-up-two-backs/ |website=Worcester News |access-date=20 January 2024 |date=23 September 2003}}</ref>

===International honours===
Davies played international rugby league for the [[Wales national rugby league team]], and played at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org (RL)"/>
